content:
The message is short, but the silence around it gives your mind enough room to build a whole incident report.

13 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Hidden Anxiety Behind Every “Can We Talk?” MessageFew workplace messages create anxiety as quickly as this one:
“Can we talk?”
It is only four words. No error stack. No failed deployment. No angry customer. No production alert. Just a short message sitting in Slack, Teams, email, or whatever tool your company uses to interrupt deep work at exactly the wrong time.
The rational part of you knows it might be harmless. Maybe someone wants to clarify a requirement. Maybe a teammate wants to pair on a bug. Maybe your manager wants to discuss priorities. Maybe a product person wants context on a tradeoff. Maybe someone simply finds it easier to explain the topic on a call.
But the anxious part of your brain rarely waits for context.
It starts building possibilities immediately. Did I break something? Was my pull request worse than I thought? Did someone complain about my work? Is this about performance? Is this about the incident last week? Did I say something wrong in the standup? Did the release fail because of my change? Is this one of those conversations people schedule when they already know the…
